title: Daily Briefing — May 20
headline: Early cracks beneath the surface as tech holds up, but defensive flows start circling.
body: Right, so let's look at what the flows are telling us: SPY grinds higher (+0.4%) but breadth is getting thin, with lagging industrials (XLI -1.2%), materials (XLB -2.4%), and discretionary (XLY -1.1%) flashing red. Bonds (HYG $79.35, LQD $107.12, TLT $83.02) remain heavy—TLT triggered below all key MAs, so per rule: if bonds freak out, everything breaks. Tech strength (AAPL, NVDA, XLK) props up the tape, but there's definite rotation brewing.

Sector view: Energy (XLE) is the only clear leader (RS=105.3, price $61.29 well above all MAs), while defensives like staples (XLP), healthcare (XLV), and utilities (XLU) are quietly improving—classic pre-risk-off profile. Financials (XLF $51.10) are wobbling near support (SMA200 $52.26) and that's the canary—keep an eye there for any flush.

Contrarian lens: Greed is up but not extreme (F&G 59.3), VIX is ticking higher, and mega-cap tech MAs are stretched (AAPL $299 vs $284.26 20MA, NVDA $220.61 vs $212.35 20MA)—don't chase extended leaders. This market smells like mid-2021: buy-the-dip crowd is crowding, but flows are rotating to defense, not confirmation of a new bull leg. Patience, react, don’t predict.
callout_body:
SPY support: $726.78 (20MA), $733.73 (current)
Tech floors: NVDA $212.35 (20MA), AAPL $284.26 (20MA)
XLF critical: $51.10 (current), $52.26 (200MA support)
TLT broke: $83.02 (well below all MAs, risk-off trigger)
Watch Energy: XLE $61.29 leading, well above all MAs
action_items: Reduce equity exposure on TLT/HYG breakdown (R001), especially industrials/materials/discretionary.
Trail stops up tight in tech leaders—don’t chase (NVDA, AAPL both stretched above 20MA); focus on relative strength in energy and defensives if adding.
closing_quote: If it’s in the press, it’s in the price. Abundance mindset—next trade.
